description: Implement the Big-M computation and arc-cost bound check used for overflow-safe reduced-cost arithmetic.
commit: feat: add Big-M computation and arc-cost overflow precondition check
steps: Create bigm.go in the repo root with `// SPDX-License-Identifier: BSL-1.0` as line 1 and `package mcf`.

Implement unexported `func bigM(n int) int64` returning `math.MaxInt64 / (8 * int64(n+1))`. Add a comment explaining: the `(n+1)` factor bounds potential accumulation along the longest tree path; the `/8` factor leaves headroom for reduced-cost subtraction. Used as the cost of artificial arcs so they strictly dominate any real arc's reduced cost unless the problem is infeasible.

Implement unexported `func arcCostWithinBound(cost int64, n int) bool` returning true iff `abs(cost) * int64(n+1) < math.MaxInt64 / 8`. Use a multiplication guard (compute absCost as int64 of the absolute value, then check `absCost > (math.MaxInt64/8) / int64(n+1)` and invert) to avoid the bound check itself overflowing. Document inline that this is precisely the precondition stated in the spec.
Assume `n >= 1`; callers perform the broader `n >= 2` check elsewhere. Do not panic.

Create bigm_test.go with: (a) TestBigMMonotone asserts bigM(2) > bigM(1000) > 0; (b) TestArcCostBoundAccepts: for n=100, cost=1_000_000 is within bound; (c) TestArcCostBoundRejects: cost = math.MaxInt64 is rejected; (d) TestBigMDominatesRealCost: for n=1000 and any cost c with `arcCostWithinBound(c, n)`, assert `int64(n+1)*abs(c) < bigM(n)` (this is the invariant the precondition guarantees).
acceptance_criteria: bigm.go and bigm_test.go compile with the SPDX header.
`arcCostWithinBound` does not itself overflow on any int64 input.
All four tests in bigm_test.go pass.
